title: Nature paper tests an autonomous medical AI agent inside a sandboxed EHR
friendlyTitle: Researchers tested an AI medical agent in a fake hospital record system
summary: A Nature paper introduced MIRA, an autonomous medical AI agent that operates in a sandboxed, FHIR-compatible electronic health record environment. In retrospective simulations on MIMIC-IV cases, the system could gather history, order tests, interpret results, propose diagnoses, and recommend structured clinical actions under constraints.
friendlySummary: The MIRA system acted inside a safe test version of an electronic health record. It could ask questions, order tests, read results, and suggest next steps for patient cases.
whyItMatters: The study points beyond medical chatbots toward governed clinical copilots that work inside real workflows. It is still simulation-based, but it clarifies what hospitals will need to evaluate: safety, permissions, auditability, and physician oversight around AI actions.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows what future doctor-assistant AI might look like. It also shows why careful testing and human supervision are essential.
